title: CI failure
body:
The below failure happens on Skopeo master for a while and is also blocking c/image.

I'm looking into it but help is appreciated.

@rhatdan @mtrmac @QiWang19 @edsantiago 

```
FAIL: copy_test.go:1070: CopySuite.TestCopyVerifyingMirroredSignatures

Running skopeo copy --dest-tls-verify=false docker://busybox docker://localhost:5006/myns/mirroring-primary:unsigned

Running skopeo --policy /tmp/policy.json172747137 --registries.d /tmp/mirrored-signatures545258538/registries.d --registries-conf fixtures/registries.conf copy --src-tls-verify=false docker://localhost:5006/myns/mirroring-primary:unsigned dir:/tmp/mirrored-signatures545258538/unused-dest

Running skopeo --registries.d /tmp/mirrored-signatures545258538/registries.d copy --src-tls-verify=false --dest-tls-verify=false --sign-by personal@example.com docker://localhost:5006/myns/mirroring-primary:unsigned docker://localhost:5006/myns/mirroring-primary:direct

Running skopeo --policy /tmp/policy.json172747137 --registries.d /tmp/mirrored-signatures545258538/registries.d --registries-conf fixtures/registries.conf copy --src-tls-verify=false docker://localhost:5006/myns/mirroring-primary:direct dir:/tmp/mirrored-signatures545258538/unused-dest

Running skopeo --registries.d /tmp/mirrored-signatures545258538/registries.d copy --src-tls-verify=false --dest-tls-verify=false --sign-by personal@example.com docker://localhost:5006/myns/mirroring-primary:unsigned docker://localhost:5006/myns/mirroring-mirror:mirror-signed

Running skopeo --policy /tmp/policy.json172747137 --registries.d /tmp/mirrored-signatures545258538/registries.d --registries-conf fixtures/registries.conf copy --src-tls-verify=false docker://localhost:5006/myns/mirroring-mirror:mirror-signed dir:/tmp/mirrored-signatures545258538/unused-dest

Running skopeo --registries-conf fixtures/registries.conf copy --src-tls-verify=false docker://localhost:5006/myns/mirroring-primary:mirror-signed dir:/tmp/mirrored-signatures545258538/unused-dest

Running skopeo --policy /tmp/policy.json172747137 --registries.d /tmp/mirrored-signatures545258538/registries.d --registries-conf fixtures/registries.conf copy --src-tls-verify=false docker://localhost:5006/myns/mirroring-primary:mirror-signed dir:/tmp/mirrored-signatures545258538/unused-dest

Running skopeo copy --src-tls-verify=false docker://localhost:5006/myns/mirroring-primary:unsigned dir:/tmp/mirrored-signatures545258538/signing-temp

Running ls -laR /tmp/mirrored-signatures545258538/signing-temp

copy_test.go:1117:

    c.Logf("%s", combinedOutputOfCommand(c, "ls", "-laR", signingDir))

utils.go:52:

    c.Assert(err, check.IsNil, check.Commentf("%s", out))

... value *exec.ExitError = &exec.ExitError{ProcessState:(*os.ProcessState)(0xc00000e480), Stderr:[]uint8(nil)} ("exit status 2")

... ls: cannot access '/tmp/mirrored-signatures545258538/signing-temp': Operation not permitted
```

comments.body:
It runs just fine locally. Looking at the Travis build failures, I see it first in https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1088 which is entirely unrelated.

Could it be an Ubuntu / Travis fart?

This makes little sense at a first glance; either umask default was changed drastically, the `/tmp` directory has some special protections, or something has gone very wrong. Let’s see whether #1096 sheds any light on the matter.

#1096 ended up showing hard to explain permission errors, and #1094 commented out the relevant code.

Can anyone suggest ways to debug/fix the root issue, or do we give up and close this?
Leaving this open to perhaps reconsider after the Cirrus migration.
Is this error coming from the unit, integration, or system tests?

> Is this error coming from the unit, integration, or system tests?

Integration tests: `integration/copy_test.go`
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Not seeing the original issue anymore.

good to close or are the failures new blockers?
that was only on fedora, i'll give it a try on ubuntu as well
I think we can try reverting commit 65c5b0bf8d39 and see if it passes now on Cirrus.

> Not seeing the original issue anymore.
> 
> ```
>     s.cluster = startOpenshiftCluster(c) // FIXME: Set up TLS for the docker registry port instead of using "--tls-verify=false" all over the place.
> ```
Failure to start OpenShift, and the resulting MISS state (not tested) for most of the cases, would be a blocker (or, at the very least, a reason to give up on testing against OpenShift, and to significantly rewrite the remaining tests). But that’s almost certainly completely unrelated.

Valentin’s recommendation to try reverting the workaround is what this issue is about.

> I think we can try reverting commit [65c5b0b](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/commit/65c5b0bf8d391ab19c86c0e7d337c457369ecba5) and see if it passes now on Cirrus.

#1367 .
